live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it into the service layer.
Top-level request orchestrator for owner resolution, record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or defaults.
Loads the fallback owner from config/defaults.json when --owner is absent or blank.
Builds the base persisted record with name, status, and resolved owner only.
Builds routing_key from the resolved owner and item name after owner resolution.
Copies the base record and emits owner_source and routing_key into the exported payload.

owner_source.logic: Returns "explicit" when the owner argument is present after strip(); otherwise returns "default" with the fallback owner from config/defaults.json.

routing_key.logic: Formats "{slugified owner}:{slugified name}" using the effective owner returned by _resolve_owner.

emission.logic: serialize_payload copies the base record, then attaches owner_source and routing_key before returning the exported payload.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner yields owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing owner flag triggers default owner loading and routing_key derivation from that resolved owner.
The CLI path forwards --owner through to the same emitted payload fields as the direct service path.
Docs intentionally expose --owner, owner_source, routing_key, and sync_item as the public path surfaces.

rejected_decoys.reason: Its storage-origin and pre-CLI-routing-key suspicions conflict with the active code path in cli.main, service.sync_item, _resolve_owner, build_routing_key, and serialize_payload.
Unused helper; no live caller references it, so it does not participate in owner_source derivation.
Unused legacy helper; the active path uses make_record instead.
Unused legacy helper; the active path uses serializer.build_routing_key instead.
